rationale: Candidate X produced no answer at all (agent hit max iterations and escalated), so it scores zero on all axes. Candidate Y correctly diagnoses the root cause exactly as the thread resolved it: the test was run at the default core_model run-level, which patches stage configs to load_format:dummy (extended to online serving by PR #4354, matching the maintainer's own citation), producing garbage output rather than a real bug; it prescribes the correct fix (--run-level=full_model) and concludes 'invalid,' matching the actual closure. Y is well-grounded with specific file/line citations and even flags a reasonable ergonomics follow-up without overreaching.
